content.cn_text: 主席:我们非常高兴地欢迎我们今天的特别兴趣小组，琳达·格雷卡博士她来自城市盲人协会。琳达要跟我们谈谈盲人的书写系统，也就是盲文。琳达,欢迎。
content.en_text: Chairman: We're very pleased to welcome to our special interest group today, Dr. Linda Graycar who is from the City Institute for the Blind. Linda is going to talk to us about the system of writing for the blind known as Braille. Linda, welcome.

content.cn_text: 格雷卡博士:谢谢。
content.en_text: Dr. Graycar: Thank you.

content.cn_text: 主席:现在我们希望这次会议不那么正式，我知道Linda不会介意小组成员在我们进行的过程中提出问题。让我们从一个显而易见的例子开始。什么是盲文，它的名字从何而来?
content.en_text: Chairman: Now we'd like to keep this session pretty informal, and I know Linda won't mind if members of the group want to ask questions as we go along. Let's start with an obvious one. What is Braille and where does it get its name from?

content.cn_text: 格雷卡博士:嗯，正如你所说，盲文是一种供盲人使用的书写系统。它的名字来源于发明它的人，生活在19世纪初的法国人路易斯·布莱叶。
content.en_text: Dr. Graycar: Well, as you said, Braille is a system of writing used by and for people who cannot see. It gets its name from the man who invented it, the Frenchman Louis Braille who lived in the early 19th century.

content.cn_text: 路易斯·布莱叶真的是盲人吗?
content.en_text: Chairman: Was Louis Braille actually blind himself?

content.cn_text: 格雷卡博士:嗯……他并非天生失明，但三岁时在他父亲的车间里发生的一次事故使他双目失明。路易·布莱叶后来去了巴黎国立盲童研究所在那里他发明了自己的书写系统1824年8月22日，年仅15岁，当时他还在研究所。
content.en_text: Dr. Graycar: Well ... he wasn't born blind, but he lost his sight at the age of three as the result of an accident in his father's workshop. Louis Braille then went to Paris to the National Institute for Blind Children and that's where he invented his writing system at the age of only 15 in  1824 while he was at the Institute.

content.cn_text: 主席:但是他并不是第一个为盲人发明触摸阅读系统的人，对吗?
content.en_text: Chairman: But he wasn't the first person to invent a system of touch reading for the blind, was he?

content.cn_text: 格雷卡博士:不，另一个法国人已经想到了印刷凸版字母的想法，使其从纸张中脱颖而出，但这是非常麻烦和低效的。
content.en_text: Dr. Graycar: No - another Frenchman had already come up with the idea of printing embossed letters that stood out from the paper but this was very cumbersome and inefficient.

content.cn_text: 路易斯·布莱叶的系统是基于第一个吗?
content.en_text: Chairman: Did Louis Braille base his system on this first one?

content.cn_text: 格雷卡博士:不，不完全是。当他第一次去巴黎时，他听说了一种使用十二个点的军事书写系统。这是一种由一位有进取心的法国军官发明的系统，被称为“夜间写作”
content.en_text: Dr. Graycar: No, not really. When he first went to Paris he heard about a military system of writing using twelve dots. This was a system invented by an enterprising French army officer and it was known as ' night writing' It wasn't meant for the

content.cn_text: 是盲目的，但是……用于夜间作战通讯。
content.en_text: blind, but rather ... for battle communications at night.

content.cn_text: 主席:那一定很有趣!
content.en_text: Chairman: That must've been fun!

content.cn_text: 格雷卡博士:不管怎样，布莱叶以这个系统为起点，但他没有使用“夜间写作”使用的12个点，而是将点的数量减少了一半，开发了一个6个点的系统。
content.en_text: Dr. Graycar: Anyway, Braille took this system as a starting point but instead of using the twelve dots which 'night writing' used, he cut the number of dots in half and developed a six-dot system.

content.cn_text: 主席:你能给我们多介绍一下它的工作原理吗?
content.en_text: Chairman: Can you give us a little more information about how it works?

content.cn_text: 格雷卡博士:嗯，这是一种触摸读取系统，它使用一种叫做细胞的凸起点排列。盲文将圆点位置向左向下编号为1-2-3，向右向下编号为4-5-6。然后用这些点的不同组合形成字母表中的字母。
content.en_text: Dr. Graycar: Well, it's a system of touch reading which uses an arrangement of raised dots called a cell. Braille numbered the dot positions 1-2-3 downward on the left and 4-5-6 downward on the right. The letters of the alphabet are then formed by using different combinations of these dots.

content.cn_text: 学生:那么书写系统是基于每个单词都被单独拼出来的字母表吗?
content.en_text: Student: So is the writing system based on the alphabet with each word being individually spelt out?

content.cn_text: 格雷卡博士:嗯……恐怕事情没那么简单!例如，字母表的前10个字母由点1、2、4和5组成。但是盲文也有自己的常用词的缩略形式。例如，“b”代表“but”，“h”代表“have”——还有很多类似的缩写。
content.en_text: Dr. Graycar: Well ... it's not quite that simple, I'm afraid! For instance, the first 10 letters of the alphabet are formed using dots 1, 2, 4 and 5. But Braille also has its own short forms for common words. For example, 'b' for the word 'but' and 'h' for 'have' - there are many other contractions like this.

content.cn_text: 主席:所以大部分单词你都是一个字母一个字母拼出来的，但是常用词你用缩写形式。
content.en_text: Chairman: So you spell out most words letter by letter, but you use short forms for common words.

content.cn_text: 格雷卡博士:是的。不过，我认为这听起来比实际情况容易一些!
content.en_text: Dr. Graycar: Yes. Though, I think that makes it sound a little easier than it actually is!

content.cn_text: 主席:它被立即接受了吗?我是说，它是一开始就流行起来的吗?
content.en_text: Chairman: And was it immediately accepted? I mean, did it catch on straight away?

content.cn_text: 格雷卡博士:嗯，是也不是!布莱叶的同学们立即接受并使用了这套系统，但直到1854年布莱叶去世两年后，这套系统才被正式采用。所以，官方的认可来得很慢!
content.en_text: Dr. Graycar: Well, yes and no! It was immediately accepted and used by Braille's fellow students at the school but the system was not officially adopted until 1854, two years after Braille's death. So, official acceptance was slow in coming!

content.cn_text: 学生:我想它适用于所有使用罗马字母的语言?
content.en_text: Student: I suppose it works for all languages which use the roman alphabet?

content.cn_text: 格雷卡博士:是的，当然是有适应性的。
content.en_text: Dr. Graycar: Yes, it does, with adaptations, of course.

content.cn_text: 学生:可以手写吗?还是需要机器来制作盲文?
content.en_text: Student: Can it be written by hand or do you need a machine to produce Braille?

content.cn_text: 格雷卡博士:嗯，你可以用一种叫做石板和触笔的设备把它手写在纸上，但关键是你必须倒着写……从右到左，这样当你把纸翻过来时，圆点面朝上，就可以像从左到右读英语一样。
content.en_text: Dr. Graycar: Well, you can write it by hand on to paper with a device called a slate and stylus but the trick is that you have to write backwards ... e.g. from right to left so that then when you turn your sheet over, the dots face upwards and can be read like English from left to right.

content.cn_text: 学生:哦，我明白了。
content.en_text: Student: Oh, I see.

content.cn_text: 但现在你可能会使用盲文书写机，这要简单得多!
content.en_text: Dr. Graycar: But these days you'd probably use a Braillewriting machine, which is a lot easier!

content.cn_text: 主席:琳达，告诉我们吧。盲文还有其他用途吗?除了阅读文本?
content.en_text: Chairman: And, tell us, Linda. Is Braille used in other ways? Other than for reading text?

content.cn_text: 格雷卡博士:是的，确实如此。除了众所周知的盲文编码，当然也包括英语和法语，还有其他编码。例如，1965年，他们为数学创造了一种盲文。
content.en_text: Dr. Graycar: Yes, indeed. In addition to the literary Braille code, as it's known, which of course includes English and French, there are other codes. For instance, in 1965 they created a form of Braille for Mathematics.

content.cn_text: 学生:我无法想象用盲文做数学!
content.en_text: Student: I can't, imagine trying to do maths in Braille!

content.cn_text: 格雷卡博士:是的，这听起来确实很难，我同意。还有一个科学记数法的版本。哦，对了，我差点忘了，现在还有一个乐谱的版本。
content.en_text: Dr. Graycar: Yes, that does sound difficult, I agree. And there's also a version for scientific notation. Oh and yes, I almost forgot, there is now a version for  music notation as well.

content.cn_text: 主席:嗯，谢谢你，琳达。
content.en_text: Chairman: Well, thanks, Linda.

groups.desc:
Questions 21-23
Complete the notes below.
Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS or A NUMBER for each answer.
groups.collect:
Braille - a system of writing for the blind
Louis Braille was blinded as a child in his $16190$
Braille invented the writing system in the year $16191$
An early writing system for the blind used embossed letters.
A military system using dots was called $16192$

items.analysis: 这道题的关键在于准确理解Louis Braille失明的具体情况。原文中提到他"lost his sight at the age of three as the result of an accident in his father's workshop"，这句话直接回答了题干中问到的Louis Braille在哪里失明的问题。"father's workshop"是准确的答案，因为它明确指出了事故发生的具体地点。这里需要注意的是，虽然原文还提到了其他地点如巴黎和国立盲童学院，但这些都是Louis Braille失明之后才去的地方，不是他失明的地点。所以，正确答案必须是"father's workshop"。这个答案既符合题目要求的字数限制（不超过三个词），又准确地回答了问题。在听力考试中，准确捕捉这样的细节信息是非常重要的。

这道题的关键在于准确识别和理解听力材料中的时间信息。文中明确提到布莱叶在1824年发明了他的书写系统，这正是我们需要的答案。你需要注意区分年龄（15岁）和年份（1824年）。题目要求的是发明年份，而不是布莱叶的年龄。此外，"invented his writing system"这个短语是定位答案的关键信号词。记住，在听力题目中，数字信息通常都很重要，尤其是在问及具体日期或年份的题目中。因此，当你听到类似的信息时，要特别留心并记下来。

这道题的关键在于准确捕捉原文中提到的军事系统的名称。文中提到这个系统是由一位法国军官发明的，使用12个点进行书写，这些信息都是为了引出系统的名称。关键词"known as"（被称为）直接引出了答案"night writing"。这个名称很特别，因为它在原文中用引号标注出来，这是一个重要的信号，表明这是一个专有名词或特定术语。因此，"night writing"是这个军事系统的正式名称，也就是题目要求的答案。记住，在听力考试中，当你听到"known as"、"called"或"named"这样的词时，它们后面通常会跟着重要的名称或术语，这往往就是答案所在。

groups.desc:
Questions 24-27
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

items.title: B C
items.analysis: 由于题目内容不完整，无法提供具体的分析。正确答案是B，但没有足够的上下文来解释为什么B是正确的。

items.title: What can the combined dots represent?
items.analysis: 布莱叶点字系统的组合点既可以表示字母，又可以表示单词。文中明确指出字母表的字母是由点组成的，同时也提到了常用词的简写形式。这表明布莱叶点字系统的versatility（多功能性），可以同时表示字母和单词，因此A选项是正确答案。

items.title: When was the Braille system officially adopted?

options.title: A
options.content: as soon as it was invented

options.title: B
options.content: two years after it was invented

items.analysis: 文中明确指出布莱叶点字系统"was not officially adopted until 1854, two years after Braille's death"（直到1854年，也就是布莱叶去世两年后才正式被采用）。这直接对应了选项C "after Louis Braille had died"（在路易斯·布莱叶去世之后）。因此，C是正确答案。

items.title: What is unusual about the way Braille is written?

options.title: A
options.content: It can only be written using a machine.

options.title: B
options.content: The texts have to be read backwards.

options.title: C
options.content: Handwritten Braille is created in reverse.

items.analysis: 文中明确指出手写盲文的独特之处在于需要"倒着写"（write backwards）和"从右向左"（from right to left）。这种反向书写的方法是为了确保当纸张翻转后，凸点能正确朝上，从而可以正常从左向右阅读。这种特殊的写作方式正是盲文手写过程中最不寻常的地方，因此C选项"Handwritten Braille is created in reverse"是正确答案。

groups.title: What THREE SUBJECTS use Braille code?

collect_options.title: What THREE SUBJECTS use Braille code?

items.analysis: 文中明确提到了三种使用盲文代码的学科：数学（Mathematics）、科学（scientific notation）和音乐（music notation）。这些学科分别对应选项B（Maths）、E（Science）和C（Music）。speaker使用了"For instance"、"And there's also"和"Oh and yes, I almost forgot"这样的表达来引入这三个学科，强调了它们都是使用盲文代码的不同领域。

文中明确提到了三种使用盲文代码的学科：数学（Mathematics）、科学（scientific notation）和音乐（music notation）。这些学科分别对应选项B（Maths）、E（Science）和C（Music）。speaker使用了"For instance"、"And there's also"和"Oh and yes, I almost forgot"这样的表达来引入这三个学科，强调了它们都是使用盲文代码的不同领域。

文中明确提到了三种使用盲文代码的学科：数学（Mathematics）、科学（scientific notation）和音乐（music notation）。这些学科分别对应选项B（Maths）、E（Science）和C（Music）。speaker使用了"For instance"、"And there's also"和"Oh and yes, I almost forgot"这样的表达来引入这三个学科，强调了它们都是使用盲文代码的不同领域。
